response: The life of this influential scholar was deeply rooted in the study of the Renaissance, a period she approached with rigorous scholarly dedication. Her first major contribution involved compiling a comprehensive catalogue that redefined how scholars understood the masterpieces of the Renaissance era. Throughout her career, she published a second, even more extensive catalogue that served as the definitive guide for researchers studying the Renaissance. Her third and final catalogue solidified her reputation as the preeminent authority on the cultural rebirth of the Renaissance. Her legacy in art history is unmatched.
